title: Peltopus plicatus
taxonomy: Peltopus plicatus is a species of sea snail, specifically a species of cone snail. It belongs to the family Conidae. Conidae is a family of venomous snails that are marine gastropod mollusks. This family is further classified under the order Neogastropoda. The classification of Peltopus plicatus is also associated with the phylum Mollusca.
uses: Peltopus plicatus is not a known plant species. However, I can provide information on the medicinal uses of a plant called 'Peltophorum' or 'Peltophorum plicatum' which is a different species.  Unfortunately, the information on the medicinal uses of Peltophorum is limited, and it's primarily used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world.  Some reported uses include the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever and malaria, as well as its use in wound healing.  Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Peltophorum for these medicinal purposes.

properties: Peltopus plicatus, also known as the South African knotweed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties.
